name: Highpoint Community Bank
description_short: Michigan-based community bank serving since 1886, offering personal and business banking with FDIC insurance, local decision-making, and personalized service.
description_long: Highpoint Community Bank is a community-focused financial institution headquartered in Michigan with a history dating back to 1886. The bank has built its reputation on relationship-based banking and local community involvement, positioning itself as an alternative to larger national banks. As of March 2026, the bank is in the process of being acquired by Independent Bank Corporation, with plans to integrate operations while maintaining local branch presence and service quality. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking services. For personal customers, they provide checking and savings accounts, including a high-yield checking account earning up to 4.00% APY, 12-month CDs at 3.75% APY, and auto loans with rates as low as 5.31% APR. Business customers have access to lending products designed to support startups and business expansion. All deposits are FDIC-insured, providing federal backing and protection. The bank also offers online banking through a redesigned digital platform and mobile app access for account management. Highpoint differentiates itself through emphasis on personalized service, local decision-making authority, and strong community involvement. The bank maintains multiple physical locations across West Michigan and hosts community events like the Highpoint Club Annual Holiday Luncheon. Customer testimonials highlight the personal relationships, knowledgeable staff, and small-town banking atmosphere. The recently redesigned website reflects their commitment to transparency and convenience while maintaining accessibility features. Prospective customers should note that the bank is undergoing a merger with Independent Bank Corporation, which could impact service delivery during the transition period, though the bank commits to keeping all locations open and maintaining service continuity. The bank's strength lies in serving customers who prioritize personal relationships and community banking over the digital-first approach of larger institutions. Their competitive rates on savings products and auto loans make them viable for rate-conscious savers and borrowers.
services: Personal checking accounts with high-yield option (up to 4.00% APY)
Online banking platform with login access
Credit card products (Elan Credit Card mentioned)
Local branch locations across West Michigan
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full federal backing
High-yield checking account earning up to 4.00% APY
Auto loan rates starting as low as 5.31% APR
Established community bank with 140+ year history since 1886
Personalized service with local decision-making authority
Mobile app and redesigned online banking platform for account management
Active community involvement with events and sponsorships
cons: Currently undergoing merger with Independent Bank Corporation, which may cause service disruption or changes
Limited geographic footprint (West Michigan only) compared to national banks
Website content does not specify loan approval timelines or qualification requirements
No mention of investment products, wealth management, or advisory services beyond basic banking
Limited information about business banking specific products or SBA lending options
best_for: West Michigan residents seeking personalized banking relationships over large-bank anonymity
Savers looking for competitive rates on CDs and high-yield checking accounts
Auto loan shoppers in the Michigan region comparing rates and seeking local service
Small business owners needing relationship-based lending and community bank support
diagnosis: Highpoint Community Bank is best for West Michigan residents and business owners who value personalized service, relationship banking, and competitive rates over the convenience of large national banks or digital-only neobanks. The main caveat is that the bank is actively merging with Independent Bank Corporation as of March 2026, which presents uncertainty around potential service changes, rate adjustments, or operational disruptions during integration.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
